untranslatable_reason: This signal requires tracking multi-leg options positions (short stock + long calls with quantity ratios) and monitoring relative profitability thresholds ('stock rises far enough' or 'falls far enough'). The backtest engine cannot express: (1) position-level constraints like call_quantity_ratio > 1, (2) strike price relationships, (3) option Greeks or implied volatility, (4) multi-leg P&L triggers, or (5) options expiration monitoring. The signal is fundamentally an options strategy requiring options-specific parameters and calculations beyond price/volume/volatility indicators.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_55 describes a Reverse Hedge (Simulated Straddle) — an advanced options strategy requiring options contract management and multi-leg position tracking. Standard equity backtest engines cannot model this without options pricing modules, strike specifications, and position-level logic unavailable in the allowed indicator set.
